claim: Socket Mode uses an outbound WebSocket so the app needs no public HTTP endpoint and is firewall-friendly, but it caps at 10 concurrent connections per app and Slack recommends HTTP for production and Marketplace apps.
quote: Socket Mode is a great option if you're building an on-premise integration, have no ability to receive external HTTP requests... Slack limits the number of concurrent WebSocket connections to 10 per app... we recommend using HTTP for production applications.

claim: Apps must respond to an Events API delivery with a 2xx within 3 seconds; Slack retries up to three times (immediately, +1 min, +5 min) with x-slack-retry-num, and >95% failures in 60 min temporarily disables the subscription.
quote: Your app should respond to the event request with an HTTP 2xx within three seconds... The first retry will be sent nearly immediately. The second retry will be attempted after 1 minute. The third and final retry will be sent after 5 minutes... more than 95% of delivery attempts within 60 minutes.

claim: Slack signs every HTTP request with HMAC-SHA256 over v0:timestamp:body keyed by the signing secret, delivered in X-Slack-Signature with X-Slack-Request-Timestamp, and receivers should reject timestamps older than 5 minutes to defeat replay attacks.
quote: Every Slack request includes X-Slack-Signature and X-Slack-Request-Timestamp headers... Reject requests older than 5 minutes to block replay attacks.

claim: Slash commands enforce the same 3-second budget as events: HTTP 200 within 3000 ms or Slack surfaces an operation_timeout error to the user.
quote: Commands must receive HTTP 200 responses within 3000 milliseconds of the original request, otherwise an operation_timeout error will be displayed to the user.

claim: The official Claude Code in Slack integration only triggers on @mentions in public or private channels (no DMs), gathers thread/channel context, runs the session on claude.ai/code, and offers Code-only vs Code+Chat routing modes with a Retry-as-Code escape hatch.
quote: Claude Code in Slack only works in channels (public or private). It does not work in direct messages (DMs)... a new Claude Code session is created on claude.ai/code... Code only — Claude routes all @mentions to Claude Code sessions... Code + Chat — Claude analyzes each message and intelligently routes.

claim: A common self-hosted pattern uses Slack Socket Mode with a connections:write app-level token so the bot needs no public ingress, running Claude Code locally against a configured working directory with per-thread working-directory state.
quote: Go to 'Basic Information' → 'App-Level Tokens' [and] Generate a token with connections:write scope... Thread-Specific Working Directories.

claim: Hardened bridges combine Socket Mode with an MCP stdio server spawned by Claude Code itself, providing per-thread session isolation, policy-gated tools, and a hash-chained audit journal across Bun/Node/Docker runtimes — all behind a firewall.
quote: WebSocket (Socket Mode — outbound only, no public URL)... server.ts (local MCP server, spawned by Claude Code)... per-thread session isolation.

claim: An alternative architecture puts a thin Slack handler (Cloudflare Workers + Hono) in front of GitHub — @mentions become GitHub issues, claude-code-action runs on the GitHub Actions runner, and a Cloudflare Queue polls issue/PR comments every 10 s for up to 30 minutes and mirrors them back to the Slack thread.
quote: Cloudflare Queues: Handles asynchronous progress monitoring, 'checking for GitHub updates every 10 seconds' and stopping 'after 30 minutes or when work is complete'.

claim: The canonical way to satisfy the 3-second ack while running long Claude Code work is to ack immediately and offload to a queue worker (SQS, Cloudflare Queue, Redis stream); processing inside the same HTTP handler is explicitly discouraged.
quote: Respond to events with an HTTP 200 OK as soon as you can. Avoid actually processing and reacting to events within the same process. Implement a queue to handle inbound events after they are received.

claim: For Events-API self-hosted bots without a static public IP, Cloudflare Tunnel has become the 2026 free default over ngrok because of stable hostnames that survive restarts and avoid Slack app reconfiguration.
quote: Custom persistent subdomains on the free tier allow you to no longer need to update your Stripe, GitHub, or Slack hook settings every time you restart your machine.

claim: yuya-takeyama/cc-slack is a Go bot that exposes Claude Code via a `/cc` slash command with single- and multi-directory project modes, thread-based session continuity, and image handling; 14 stars, last pushed 2025-08-23 (modestly maintained).
quote: Interact with Claude Code on Slack. Language: Go. stargazers_count: 14. pushed_at: 2025-08-23T11:21:46Z. Slash command interface (/cc default), thread-based conversations.

claim: chenhg5/cc-connect is by far the most popular bridge (6,422 stars, last pushed 2026-04-27 — actively maintained), a Go binary that uses Slack Socket Mode (no public IP needed) plus 10 other chat platforms and 10+ agents (Claude Code, Cursor, Codex, Gemini CLI), with stateful sessions and a web admin dashboard.
quote: Bridge local AI coding agents (Claude Code, Cursor, Gemini CLI, Codex) to messaging platforms… stargazers_count: 6422. pushed_at: 2026-04-27T12:55:54Z. Uses Slack's Socket Mode connection… eliminates the need for a public-facing webhook.

claim: JessyTsui/Claude-Code-Remote (1,228 stars, JavaScript, last pushed 2025-12-06) controls Claude Code via email/Telegram/LINE/desktop using a hook + tmux/PTY command-injection mechanism; Slack is on the TODO roadmap, not yet shipped.
quote: Control Claude Code remotely via email、discord、telegram… roadmap lists 'Discord Integration' and 'Slack Workflow' as planned features under 'TODO List'. stargazers_count: 1228. pushed_at: 2025-12-06.

claim: dbenn8/claude-slack (22 stars, Python, last pushed 2026-01-31) implements bidirectional Slack ↔ Claude using a hook-based pattern — `.claude/hooks/` notifies Slack and a separate `slack_listener.py` Socket Mode bot writes replies to a shared file polled via `/check`; multi-session phase 2.5 in progress.
quote: Universal Slack integration for Claude Code - bidirectional communication, multi-session support, and real-time messaging. stargazers_count: 22. Language: Python. pushed_at: 2026-01-31T08:45:46Z.

claim: takafu/slack-claude-bot (TypeScript, 2 stars, last pushed 2026-01-16) is a thin per-thread Claude Code CLI wrapper where each Slack thread holds its own session context — a minimal but recently active reference implementation.
quote: Slack bot that integrates with Claude Code CLI for AI-powered responses. stargazers_count: 2. Language: TypeScript. pushed_at: 2026-01-16T13:54:36Z.

claim: engineers-hub-ltd's slack-claude-code-integration (JavaScript, 11 stars, last pushed 2025-06-30) wraps Claude Code as an MCP server (Slack → Bot → MCP server → Claude Code CLI) with a `/claude` slash command and `--project` flag for multi-project switching.
quote: Architecture Flow: Slack → Slack Bot → Claude Code MCP Server → Claude Code CLI… stargazers_count: 11. pushed_at: 2025-06-30T07:26:48Z.

claim: satetsu888/slackbot-for-claude (TypeScript, 5 stars, last pushed 2024-03-17) is effectively abandoned — predates Claude Code and just wraps the raw Anthropic API; included only as historical context.
quote: Slackbot that integrates with the Claude API. stargazers_count: 5. pushed_at: 2024-03-17T13:42:14Z.

claim: OpenACP is another self-hosted bridge with an interactive setup wizard supporting Telegram, Discord, and Slack simultaneously, daemon/foreground modes, and per-workspace agent defaults — targeted at users who want to drive Claude Code from any phone client without a public IP.
quote: An interactive setup wizard asks which platform you want (Telegram, Discord, Slack, or all three), your bot token, which workspace directory to use, which agent to default to, and whether to run in foreground or daemon mode.

claim: The -p / --print flag runs Claude Code non-interactively and was previously called headless mode; all CLI options work with -p including --continue, --allowedTools, and --output-format.
quote: The CLI was previously called "headless mode." The `-p` flag and all CLI options work the same way... Add the `-p` (or `--print`) flag to any `claude` command to run it non-interactively. All CLI options work with `-p`.

claim: --permission-mode accepts default, acceptEdits, plan, auto, dontAsk, or bypassPermissions and overrides defaultMode from settings files.
quote: Begin in a specified permission mode. Accepts `default`, `acceptEdits`, `plan`, `auto`, `dontAsk`, or `bypassPermissions`. Overrides `defaultMode` from settings files

claim: Sessions are persisted automatically as JSONL transcripts at ~/.claude/projects/<encoded-cwd>/<session-id>.jsonl; resuming with a mismatched cwd silently yields a fresh session.
quote: Sessions are stored under `~/.claude/projects/<encoded-cwd>/*.jsonl`, where `<encoded-cwd>` is the absolute working directory with every non-alphanumeric character replaced by `-`... If your resume call runs from a different directory, the SDK looks in the wrong place.

claim: The Python SDK's CanUseTool callback (Callable[[str, dict, ToolPermissionContext], Awaitable[PermissionResult]]) is the headless equivalent of an interactive permission prompt and returns PermissionResultAllow with optional updated_input or PermissionResultDeny with a message.
quote: CanUseTool: Callable[[str, dict[str, Any], ToolPermissionContext], Awaitable[PermissionResult]]... PermissionResult is a union that can be either PermissionResultAllow (with optional updated_input and updated_permissions) or PermissionResultDeny (with a message and optional interrupt flag).

claim: Anthropic launched Claude Code in Slack as a research preview on December 8, 2025, framed as 'delegate tasks to Claude Code directly from Slack' rather than a webhook hack.
quote: Now in beta as a research preview, Claude makes it easy to move context from Slack conversations to coding sessions... When you mention @Claude in Slack, Claude reviews your message to determine if it's a coding task. If it is, a new Claude Code session will automatically be created.

claim: The 'Claude' app on the Slack App Marketplace is the official Anthropic-published consumer app — the same app surface that Claude Code in Slack is built on top of.
quote: Transform your work day with Claude, your thoughtful AI collaborator in Slack. Draft an email, summarize a document, brainstorm ideas, or get fast answers.

claim: The 'Claude in Slack' help-center article frames the integration as Claude.ai chat plus Claude Code routing — i.e. it is both the consumer chat surface and the dev-task entry point, not two separate apps.
quote: Claude in Slack gives you AI assistance right where your team collaborates.

claim: Anthropic's Claude Code Routines (research preview, April 14 2026) are the official 'remote-trigger Claude Code from outside' surface and natively support Schedule, API (per-routine bearer-token /fire endpoint), and GitHub-event triggers — but Slack is not a first-class trigger; Slack appears only as an MCP connector for the routine to read/write during a run.
quote: Each routine can have one or more triggers attached to it: Scheduled... API: trigger on demand by sending an HTTP POST to a per-routine endpoint with a bearer token... GitHub: run automatically in response to repository events

claim: anthropics/claude-code-action v1 is officially triggered by GitHub events (issue_comment, pull_request_review_comment, issues opened/assigned, schedule, workflow_dispatch) and reacts to '@claude' mentions; the docs show no first-party Slack trigger — Slack-to-GHA bridges remain user-built via repository_dispatch.
quote: on: issue_comment: types: [created] pull_request_review_comment: types: [created] issues: types: [opened, assigned] ... Responds to @claude mentions in comments

claim: The official anthropics/claude-code-action repo (7,317 stars) is the supported GitHub Action and supports Anthropic API, AWS Bedrock, Google Vertex AI, and Microsoft Foundry as backends — but its trigger surface is GitHub events, not Slack.
quote: A general-purpose Claude Code action for GitHub PRs and issues that can answer questions and implement code changes. This action intelligently detects when to activate based on your workflow context—whether responding to @claude mentions, issue assignments, or executing automation tasks with explicit prompts.

claim: Slack itself (not Anthropic) ships and maintains the official Slack MCP server, GA on February 17 2026; Anthropic is a partner consumer of it and the connector is what Claude.ai/Claude Code uses to read/write Slack.
quote: We continue to deepen our integration with Slack through our MCP Apps extension and by including the power of Claude Code.

claim: The original modelcontextprotocol/servers-archived reference Slack server (parent repo 84,644 stars) was archived May 29, 2025; @modelcontextprotocol/server-slack on npm is no longer the canonical path — Slack's own MCP server superseded it.
quote: This repository was archived by the owner on May 29, 2025. It is now read-only.

claim: Slack truncates messages over 40,000 characters and returns a message_truncated warning, with a recommendation to keep text under 4,000 characters and upload snippets for longer payloads.
quote: Slack will truncate messages containing more than 40,000 characters... For best results, limit the number of characters in the text field to 4,000 characters... If you need to post longer messages, we recommend uploading a snippet instead.

claim: Slack Bolt users hit operation_timeout errors on slash commands even when ack() is called immediately, and partial delivery occurs for bulk operations to over 100 users.
quote: All the DMs are sent properly, but the user receives operation_timeout error message... When attempting to message over 100 users, partial delivery occurs alongside the timeout error.

claim: Slack Bolt for Python introduced 'lazy listeners' specifically because slash command handlers must return an HTTP 200 within 3 seconds, decoupling the ack from any long-running work.
quote: Lazy listener functions are responsible for returning an immediate HTTP response to Slack API servers within 3 seconds, while lazy functions can do anything... completely free from 3-second timeouts.

claim: A Claude Code Slack bot guide names the 3-second ack rule as the most critical timing constraint and prescribes the ack-then-respond pattern, but does not address prompt injection, cost runaway, or sandboxing.
quote: Slack requires a response within 3 seconds. The solution is to acknowledge immediately with ack() then send data asynchronously via respond(). The generated code already handles this pattern.

claim: Mitiga researchers demonstrated a working prompt-injection attack where a malicious Claude skill weaponizes Claude Code's Slack tools to phish coworkers from the user's own account.
quote: once the agent is abused, the campaign fully inherits the trust, tone, and reach of the user's Slack account... If direct targeting fails, spread the link to public channels instead.

claim: Claude Code cannot safely connect to Slack via MCP for enterprise users because it requires OAuth 2.0 Dynamic Client Registration which Slack's MCP server does not support, forcing admins to manually create an internal Slack app and pass --client-id/--client-secret.
quote: Claude Code requires DCR for HTTP-based MCP server authentication, but Slack's MCP server doesn't support it... I can't trust anything my company says on Slack being exposed to a third party.

claim: Claude Code's native sandbox uses OS-level filesystem and network isolation; without network isolation a compromised agent could exfiltrate SSH keys, and without filesystem isolation it could backdoor system resources — relevant for any Slack bot running tools on behalf of arbitrary users.
quote: The sandboxed bash tool uses OS-level primitives to enforce both filesystem and network isolation. Without network isolation, a compromised agent could exfiltrate sensitive files like SSH keys.

claim: Practitioners running AI agents 24/7 warn that without runtime budget enforcement a loop bug can generate $2,000 in API charges in under an hour — directly relevant to a Slack bot where any user can @-mention.
quote: Runaway agents burn money fast. Without runtime budget enforcement, a loop bug can generate $2,000 in API charges in under an hour.

claim: Claude Code, Gemini CLI, and GitHub Copilot agents have all been shown vulnerable to prompt injection from untrusted text in tickets, comments, and chat messages — the same threat surface any Slack bot exposes.

Truefoundry's enterprise guidance explicitly calls out that Claude Code wired into MCP tools with broad permissions can be manipulated into using those tools outside the original task, and recommends least-privilege tool access as the primary mitigation for chat-driven agents.
quote: Claude Code connected to MCP servers with broad permissions can be manipulated into using those tools outside the original task... requiring least-privilege tool access as primary mitigation.

claim: 41fred/claude-code-slack splits the system into a FastAPI worker on Railway (handles Slack HMAC-SHA256 signature verification and the 3s ack, writes task JSON files into a tasks/ directory in a GitHub repo) and a local macOS daemon that polls GitHub every 5s, runs `claude -p` in an isolated cloned workspace, and posts results — using GitHub itself as the queue.
quote: The worker validates every incoming request using 'HMAC-SHA256 signature' ... Worker writes JSON task file to tasks/ directory ... Daemon polls and detects new files every 5 seconds ... Claude operates within a 'separate clone' workspace directory ... Workspace changes trigger automatic Git commits and pushes after each task completes.

claim: MattKilmer/claude-autofix-bot uses Node 20 + TypeScript + Express on Railway with a webhook at /api/slack-events; it shells out to the Claude Code CLI for full agentic explore/edit/test, uses simple-git plus the GitHub API to push a semantic branch and open a PR, and supports image attachments by downloading Slack file URLs and feeding them to Claude.
quote: The bot receives Slack events via webhooks at /api/slack-events ... invokes 'Claude Code CLI' with 'full agentic capabilities (explore, edit, run tests)' ... simple-git for local operations and GitHub API integration ... 'Screenshot Support: Attach images for visual context—Claude analyzes them'.

claim: David Calvert's 'Hive Ix' SRE Slackbot is <2,000 lines of Go deployed via Helm on GCP/Kubernetes, calling Claude Sonnet 4.5 through Vertex AI Model Garden by literally exec-ing `claude -p prompt`, with MCP servers (Atlassian, Datadog) attached for incident-response context.
quote: Language: Go (less than 2,000 lines) ... Infrastructure: Kubernetes with Helm charts ... Claude integration: Via 'Vertex AI Model Garden' with Sonnet 4.5 ... cmd := exec.Command('claude', '-p', prompt) ... MCP servers (Atlassian, Datadog).

claim: tomeraitz/claude-slack-bridge inverts the pattern: it is an MCP server that lets a running Claude Code task pause and ask a human in Slack via a daemon holding one Socket Mode WebSocket plus a Unix-domain-socket server (/tmp/slack-bridge.sock) that routes thread_ts replies to the blocked session — useful for human-in-the-loop steps inside a longer Claude run.
quote: the daemon (persistent Docker container) holds one Slack Socket Mode WebSocket connection and a Unix domain socket server that receives all Slack reply events and routes them to the correct waiting session ... session connects to /tmp/slack-bridge.sock and registers, then blocks without polling.
